---
title: "gaulss_fit"
generated: true
description: "Mass-univariate Gaussian location-scale regression (`gaulss`)."
---

[‚Äπ back to `nitrix.stats.gaulss`](../nitrix-stats-gaulss.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gaulss_fit -->
```python
gaulss_fit(Y: Float[Array, 'V N'], X: Float[Array, 'N p'], *, scale_design: Optional[Float[Array, 'N q']] = None, n_iter: int = 50, ridge: float = 1e-08, block: Optional[int] = None) -> GauLSSResult
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gaulss_fit -->
Mass-univariate Gaussian location-scale regression (`gaulss`).

Fits, per element, $y_v \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu, \sigma^2)$ with
$\mu = X \beta_{\mu}$ and $\log \sigma = Z \beta_{\sigma}$ --
modelling heteroscedasticity directly -- by joint Fisher scoring.  The mean
and log-scale designs are shared across elements; only the responses vary.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gaulss_fit -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `Y` | `Float` | `V` √ó `N` | ‚Äî | Responses, one length-`N` observation vector per element. |
| `X` | `Float` | `N` √ó `p` | ‚Äî | Mean-model design matrix, shared across elements; carries its own intercept. |
| `scale_design` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N q']]` | ‚Äî | `None` | Log-scale design matrix $Z$, shared across elements. Defaults to an intercept-only column -- a constant variance (the homoscedastic Gaussian, recovering OLS for the mean); pass a design to model the variance. |
| `n_iter` | `int` | ‚Äî | `50` | Number of Fisher-scoring iterations. Default `50`. |
| `ridge` | `float` | ‚Äî | `1e-08` | Small stabiliser added to the normal equations. Default `1e-8`. |
| `block` | `Optional[int]` | ‚Äî | `None` | Element-block size bounding peak memory; `None` processes all elements at once. |

**Returns** `GauLSSResult`. Fit output holding the mean coefficients `coef_mu`, the log-scale coefficients `coef_scale`, the block information inverses `cov_mu` and `cov_scale`, and the maximised log-likelihood `log_lik`. A mean contrast is $c^{\top} \mathrm{coef\_mu}$ with standard error $\sqrt{c^{\top} \mathrm{cov\_mu}\, c}$; a scale contrast is the analogous read-out on `coef_scale`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:gaulss_fit -->
### See also

gaulss_predict : Evaluate the fitted mean and scale on a (new) design.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:gaulss_fit -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map ‚Äî voxels, vertices, or fixels |
| `N` | observations ‚Äî time points, subjects, or sampled points |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
